description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a boxy, wooden cabinet and four slender, tapered legs, reminiscent of mid-20th-century designs. The front features a large, convex glass screen set within a recessed frame, flanked by a pair of metallic control knobs and a central tuning dial below the screen. The sides and top are finished in a dark wood veneer, giving it a classic, retro appearance. The back panel displays a label with the text 'DEEP IMAGE RADIO TELEVISION' and additional technical markings, as well as a protruding circular component, likely for ventilation or cable connections. The overall construction suggests a combination of wood, metal, and glass, with a hollow interior to house electronic components. This television would typically be found in living rooms, garages, or as a decorative piece in urban vintage-themed spaces.
description: A vintage wooden television set with a convex glass screen, metallic control knobs, and a labeled back panel, standing on four tapered legs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the convex glass screen, two metallic control knobs, and a central tuning dial below the screen.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the boxy wooden cabinet and the depth of the screen's protrusion.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the 'DEEP IMAGE RADIO TELEVISION' label, a large circular component, and technical markings.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, emphasizing the wooden finish and the overall depth of the cabinet.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, brown wood, matte finish, rectangular with metallic accents
attribute_control_button_layout: front panel, row arrangement, three visible controls (two knobs and one central dial)
